title: gowin: -nolutram needed for simple initialised memory read/write module, otherwise  spurious writes corrupt values on startup
body:
### Version

Yosys 0.26+62 (git sha1 bb28e4813, clang 13.0.0 -fPIC -Os)

### On which OS did this happen?

macOS

### Reproduction Steps

The following simple (and not totally collision-safe) SystemVerilog  memory reader/writer is interfaced via its buses to an i2c module, and acts as its 16-byte register file.

The module is iniatalised with a static file containing 0xdeadbeef, 0xbadf00d, etc. The first byte is 0xde.

This works perfectly on ice40 and if -nolutram is specied in synth_gowin. 

If this is not specified, spurious writes overwrite staitc content with 0s, (and actual writes don't work either) unless the BAD_MEMORY option is defined, at which point reads from memory work ok, except for the first byte, which winds up being the reset-initialised data from the 'data' register. This is 0xee right now but was originally found to be 0x0 on testing.

BAD_MEMORY basically captures address and data  in registers one clock cycle after write goes high and does the actual write the next  cycle after that.

Is this just bad design or is there something more fundamental going on. I can't work out a way to fix it.
```

module mem_access (
	input logic  [7:0] rw_addr,
	input logic  [7:0] d_i,
	output logic [7:0] d_o,
        input write,
	input clk,
	input reset
);

  logic  [7:0] temp_mem [16:0];
  initial $readmemh("deadbeef.hex", temp_mem);
  

  logic [7:0] addr;
  logic [7:0] data;


  logic [2:0] write_count;
  logic write_active;

  always @ (posedge clk) begin
    if (reset) begin
      write_count <= 0;
      data <= 8'hee;
      write_active <= 0;
    end else begin 
`ifdef BAD_MEMORY
	if (write && !write_active) begin
		write_active <= 1; 
	end else if(write_active && write_count == 0) begin
		addr <= rw_addr;
		data <= d_i; 
		write_count <= 1; 
		write_active <= 0; 
	end else if (write_count == 1) begin
		temp_mem[addr[3:0]] <= data;
		write_count <= 0;
        end else if (!write) begin
		d_o <= temp_mem[rw_addr[3:0]];
	end 
`else
	if (write)
		temp_mem[rw_addr[3:0]] <= d_i;
	else
		d_o <= temp_mem[rw_addr[3:0]];
`endif
     end
  end
      
endmodule

```
### Expected Behavior

Memory module outputs original initialised contents of memory.

de ad be ef
ba ad f0 0d
ba ad ca fe
fe ed ba ad 

### Actual Behavior

Only on gowin gw1n-9c. Ice40 works ok either way.

All 0's (no `define BAD_MEMORY 1) or original contents with first byte replaced by 0xee is retrieved. 

e.g.

00 00 00 00 ... etc.

OR (`define BAD_MEMORY 1)

ee ad be ef
ba ad f0 0d
ba ad ca fe
fe ed ba ad 

behaviour requires -nolutram to be corrected. both variants then read and write memory correctly.

Write also does not work unless BAD_MEMORY is specified without -nolutram.

behaviour for reads is always correct if actual write assignment is commented out prior to compiling.
